title: 2025 Session 6 exercises
body:
Session 6: Exercises


Question 1
A Swiss portfolio manager has a significant portion of the portfolio invested in US dollar-
denominated assets. The money manager is worried about the political situation
surrounding the next U.S. presidential election and fears a potential drop in the value of
the dollar. The manager decides to sell the dollars forward against Swiss francs.
a. Give some reasons why the Swiss money manager should use futures rather than
   forward currency contracts?
b. Give some reasons why the Swiss money manager should use forward currency
   contracts rather than futures?

Question 2

A five-year currency swap involves two AAA borrowers and has been set at current market
interest rates. The swap is for USD 100 million against AUD 200 million at the current spot
exchange rate of AUD/USD 2.00. The effective interest rates are 4% in U.S. dollars and 7%
in Australian dollars, or annual swaps of USD 4 million for AUD 14 million. A year later,
the interest rates have dropped to 3% in U.S. dollars and 6% in Australian dollars, and the
exchange rate is now AUD/USD 1.9.


What should the market value of the swap be in the secondary market?



Question 3
You work for a US firm that will have to pay a British customer £5 million in the next 3
months. To hedge the exchange rate risk, you can trade an option on the £. Your bank
quotes you an option premium of $0.0220 per £, for an exercise price of K = 1.50 $/£.

    a. What type of options should you buy?


    b. What is the cost incurred today?

    c. What is the ceiling that you have set on the price of the £? In other words, you
       know for sure that each £ will not costly more X$. What is this value?



                                            1
     d. What is the total amount that you will pay if the spot rate at the end of 3
       months is 1.46 $/£?

    e. What is the total amount that you will pay if the spot rate at the end of 3
       months is 1.6 $/£?

    f. How much do you save in that case?


Question 4
On Monday morning, an investor takes a long position in a pound futures contract that
matures on Wednesday afternoon. The agreed upon price is $1.78 for £62,500. At the
close of trading on Monday, the futures price has risen to $1.79. At Tuesday close, the
price rises further to $1.80. At Wednesday close, the price falls to $1.785, and the
contract matures. The investor takes delivery of the pounds at the prevailing price of
$1.785. Detail the daily settlement process (see Exhibit 8.3). What will be the investor's
profit (loss)?


Question 5
Suppose that DEC buys a Swiss franc futures contract (contract size is SFr 125,000) at a
price of $0.83. If the spot rate for the Swiss franc at the date of settlement is SFr 1 =
$0.8250, what is DEC's gain or loss on this contract?


Question 6
Citigroup sells a call option on euros (contract size is €500,000) at a premium of $0.04
per euro. If the exercise price is $0.91 and the spot price of the euro at the date of
expiration is $0.93, what is Citigroup's profit (loss) on the call option?




                                             2
